content.problemStatement:
In this problem, you will define a Django model called Student for a university registration system.\
Your task is to apply appropriate built-in field validations for each field in the model.

### Task
Create a model called Student with the following fields:

| **Field Name** | **Type**     | **Validations**                                                                          |
|----------------|--------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| name           | CharField    | Required, max_length=100                                                                 |
| email          | EmailField   | Required, must be unique                                                                 |
| roll_number    | CharField    | Required, max_length=10, must be unique                                                  |
| course         | CharField    | Choices: CS (Computer Science), EE (Electrical Engineering), ME (Mechanical Engineering) |
| year_of_study  | IntegerField | Must be between 1 and 4 (inclusive)                                                      |
| profile_url    | URLField     | Optional                                                                                 |

Complete the `Student` model in the `models.py` with the above fields & validations.

Click on **RUN** which will run migrations and start the server. \
On the hompage that opens click on `Add Students` which will attempt to add Student instances from the views.py file - out of which 2 are valid and 2 are invalid. 

See if they are handled properly. The correct expected output is as shown below.
